pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 25 defines INPUT_FILE at this exact path; file is created/verified to exist at this location before task execution
export_result.sh line 21 defines EXPECTED_PATH at this location; referenced in task.json metadata and task description

task.json description explicitly states 'Horizontal spacing: 0.1 degrees, Vertical spacing: 0.1 degrees'; export_result.sh validates cells are 0.09-0.11 degrees (lines 126); verifier.py checks cell_size_correct with this tolerance
task.json description line 4 states 'Grid CRS: EPSG:4326 (WGS 84)'; QGIS Create Grid tool documentation confirms it accepts Grid CRS parameter defaulting to project CRS

verifier.py line 73 checks 'if 10 <= count <= 40'; theoretical expected grid is 6 columns (0.6 deg / 0.1 deg) x 3 rows (0.3 deg / 0.1 deg) = 18 cells; min_feature_count of 10 allows buffer for grid tool variations
verifier.py line 73 checks 'if 10 <= count <= 40'; max_feature_count of 40 provides upper bound for grid cell count validation

privileged_info_summary: The create_vector_grid task uses a synthetic rectangular study area polygon (created from -122.5 to -121.9 longitude and 37.5 to 37.8 latitude) to generate a rectangular vector grid using QGIS's Create Grid tool with 0.1 degree horizontal and vertical spacing in EPSG:4326 (WGS 84) coordinate system. The input polygon is stored at /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_polygon.geojson, and the output grid is expected at /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/study_area_grid.geojson. The specified extent and spacing parameters produce approximately 18 rectangular polygon cells (6 columns × 3 rows, each 0.1 × 0.1 degrees), with validation tolerating feature counts between 10 and 40 cells.
